records.ety: [L. contradictio answer, objection: cf. F. contradiction.]

defs: An assertion of the contrary to what has been said or affirmed; denial of the truth of a statement or assertion; contrary declaration; gainsaying.
quotes: His fair demands Shall be accomplished without contradiction.

defs: Direct opposition or repugnancy; inconsistency; incongruity or contrariety; one who, or that which, is inconsistent.
quotes: can he make deathless death? That were to make Strange contradiction.
We state our experience and then we come to a manly resolution of acting in contradiction to it.
Both parts of a contradiction can not possibly be true.
notes: It develops itself in three specific forms which have been called the “Three Logical Axioms.” First, “A is A.” Second, “A is not Not-A” Third, “Everything is either A or Not-A.”
